untranslatable_reason: This signal requires quantification of subjective, non-price factors: (1) Pattern repetition count and historical matching cannot be measured without external pattern library/classification system; (2) Trader observation hours are metadata outside price/volume data; (3) Conviction score is a subjective trader input not derivable from technical indicators; (4) Pattern similarity scoring (75% threshold) requires pre-defined pattern templates and ML/distance metric not available in indicator set. The rule is fundamentally qualitative and requires manual trader judgment rather than algorithmic conditions.
translation_notes: SCHWAGER_DRY_0_1 emphasizes conviction-building through repeated pattern observation and extensive pre-trade analysis. While the source text fragment mentions 'developed an opinion' over time, the actual entry/exit mechanics depend entirely on: (a) pattern recognition requiring external pattern database, (b) trader-assigned conviction scores (subjective), and (c) observation period tracking (metadata). No combination of the allowed price/volume/technical indicators can operationalize these conditions without the conviction scoring system and pattern library. Recommend implementing this as a manual trade approval gate or custom pattern classification module rather than pure technical signal.
